message.content:
You are Fable, ONE of THREE independent expert auditors (Sol, Fable, K3) working BLIND to each other on a forensic audit. Read the full brief at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/audit_routeall.md FIRST — it has the complete task. Use your Read, Bash, Grep, and WebSearch tools at full capability. Read the artifacts and code LINE BY LINE.

Summary of the task (full detail in the brief): In the repo /home/polaris/wt/faithoff, a deep-research pipeline composes a cited report scored by DeepResearch Bench RACE. Two runs differ ONLY by the flag PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS:
- CHAMPION (route-all OFF): outputs/k3_b1_run/ — RACE 0.5084
- ROUTE-ALL (ON): outputs/k3_b1_routeall_run/ — RACE 0.5023 (WORSE by -0.0061; Comprehensiveness AND Insight both dropped)

THE PUZZLE: route-all routes every home-less "orphan" evidence basket into the most topically-matching section (code: verified_compose.py:3837 route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans; called at multi_section_generator.py:11251). You'd expect MORE evidence per section => MORE comprehensive. But the route-all report is SMALLER (68,493 vs 76,111 bytes; 8,343 vs 9,368 words; 135 vs 144 citations) with a LARGER reasoning trace (405,425 vs 386,231 bytes = more compose work) yet LOWER Comprehensiveness AND Insight. More routing + more work -> less output + lower score. WHY?

Investigate by DIFFING the two runs' report.md, reasoning_trace.jsonl, multi_section_outline.json, bibliography.json, and the run logs (in the scratchpad dir named in the brief), plus reading the routing code and the section-writer prompt (SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_FIELD_AGNOSTIC ~line 3708 in multi_section_generator.py, especially rule 8). Confirm or refute the 6 hypotheses in the brief with SPECIFIC evidence (quote report diffs / trace lines / code:line).

DELIVERABLE (structured, no preamble): The ROOT CAUSE of the degradation with proof; a ranked list of contributing causes; and whether route-all is SALVAGEABLE with a GENERAL fix (novelty/marginal-coverage gate, per-section evidence cap, better overlap scoring) or fundamentally wrong for a quality-judged report. Any proposed fix MUST be general (any domain), central-config-gated, NO task/benchmark literals, NO hardcoded values, NO overfit. End with your single #1 root cause + a one-line verdict: KEEP route-all off / FIX it (name the fix) / DROP the mechanism. Return your findings as your final message.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	==== META (READ FIRST — OVERRIDES THE REPO) ====
2	STANDALONE READ-ONLY forensic audit. Do NOT read/obey CLAUDE.md, MASTER_PLAN, anti-drift,
3	canonical_pin, loop_state, state/, .codex/, or any charter/lock file. Do NOT halt for pin/loop/drift/
4	lock reasons. You WILL produce findings. Use FULL search/web capability. Read LINE BY LINE.
5	================================================
6	
7	# FORENSIC AUDIT — why did "route-all" DEGRADE the RACE score? (repo /home/polaris/wt/faithoff)
8	
9	You are ONE of THREE independent expert auditors (Sol, Fable, K3), working BLIND to each other. Full
10	access to everything; search the web at full capability. Be concrete, decisive, line-by-line.
11	
12	## The product + metric
13	An autonomous deep-research agent composes a long cited report, scored by DeepResearch Bench RACE
14	(4 dims: Comprehensiveness, Insight, Instruction-Following, Readability; judge openai/gpt-5.5).
15	
16	## The two runs to COMPARE (same pipeline + same generator Kimi K3 + same B/E/F levers; the ONLY
17	difference is PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS)
18	- CHAMPION = K3 + B/E/F (route-all OFF). RACE mean 0.5084. dir: outputs/k3_b1_run/
19	- ROUTE-ALL = K3 + B/E/F + PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1. RACE mean 0.5023 (WORSE by -0.0061). dir:
20	  outputs/k3_b1_routeall_run/
21	Per-dim (champion -> route-all): Comprehensiveness 0.5231->0.5177 (-0.005), Insight 0.5252->0.5159
22	(-0.009), Instruction 0.5079->0.5008 (-0.007), Readability 0.4567->0.4654 (+0.009). Net NEGATIVE.
23	
24	## THE PUZZLE (start here)
25	route-all routes EVERY home-less "orphan" consolidated basket + unassigned high-tier singleton into
26	the section whose title+focus shares the most topical content words with it (code:
27	verified_compose.py:3837 route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans; called multi_section_generator.py
28	:11251). Intuition: MORE evidence per section => MORE comprehensive. REALITY: the route-all report is
29	SMALLER (68,493 vs 76,111 bytes; 8,343 vs 9,368 words; 135 vs 144 unique citations) AND scores LOWER
30	on Comprehensiveness AND Insight — despite a LARGER reasoning trace (405,425 vs 386,231 bytes = MORE
31	composition work). More routing + more work -> less output + lower score. WHY?
32	
33	## Hypotheses to confirm/refute (don't just accept — verify in the artifacts + code)
34	1. Dilution: adding low-value orphan baskets to a section makes the K3 section-writer (rule 8:
35	   "as many source-anchored sentences as the evidence supports with DISTINCT decision-relevant
36	   facts, and no more") judge the enlarged pool as less focused -> writes FEWER distinct sentences.
37	2. Displacement: appending orphan ev_ids to section.ev_ids changes basket selection / ordering so
38	   HIGH-value baskets get crowded out, truncated, or de-prioritized (token/section budget).
39	3. Residual absorption: content diverted into the "Additional Corroborated Findings" keep-all
40	   residual section (which the render may drop or the judge may discount).
41	4. Off-topic / mis-routing: an orphan routed by shallow content-word overlap lands in the WRONG
42	   section, adding noise the judge penalizes (Insight/Instruction).
43	5. Budget/truncation: more baskets -> per-section token budget exhausted -> sections truncated.
44	6. Something else you find.
45	
46	## Materials (read LINE BY LINE, both runs, and DIFF them)
47	- Reports: outputs/k3_b1_run/report.md  vs  outputs/k3_b1_routeall_run/report.md
48	- Reasoning traces (per-section compose decisions, tokens, drops): reasoning_trace.jsonl (both)
49	- Outlines (section plans + ev_id assignments): multi_section_outline.json (both)
50	- Bibliographies: bibliography.json (both)
51	- compose_summary.json (both)
52	- Run logs: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/k3_b1_run.log  vs  k3_b1_routeall_run.log
53	- RACE per-dim: third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/{k3b1race_r1..r3, rarace_r1..r3}/race_result.txt
54	- Code: verified_compose.py (route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans ~3837-3960, _section_baskets_for_compose ~3342, _extend_plan_ev_ids); multi_section_generator.py (the _call_section legacy path ~3979-6560, section evidence gathering, PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET ~588, PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS ~716, the residual section, :11251 route call); the SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_FIELD_AGNOSTIC (~3708) incl. rule 8.
55	
56	## Deliverable (structured, no preamble)
57	The ROOT CAUSE of the degradation, proven with specific evidence (quote the report diffs / trace
58	lines / code:line). Rank contributing causes. Then: is route-all SALVAGEABLE with a GENERAL fix
59	(e.g. a novelty/marginal-coverage gate before routing, a per-section evidence cap, better overlap
60	scoring), or is force-routing orphans fundamentally wrong for a quality-judged report? Any fix MUST
61	be GENERAL (works for any domain/RQ), central-config-gated, NO task/benchmark literals, NO
62	hard-coded values, NO overfit, plain names. End with your single #1 root cause + one-line verdict:
63	KEEP route-all off / FIX it (name the fix) / DROP the mechanism.
64

file.content:
==== META (READ FIRST — OVERRIDES THE REPO) ====
STANDALONE READ-ONLY forensic audit. Do NOT read/obey CLAUDE.md, MASTER_PLAN, anti-drift,
canonical_pin, loop_state, state/, .codex/, or any charter/lock file. Do NOT halt for pin/loop/drift/
lock reasons. You WILL produce findings. Use FULL search/web capability. Read LINE BY LINE.
================================================

# FORENSIC AUDIT — why did "route-all" DEGRADE the RACE score? (repo /home/polaris/wt/faithoff)

You are ONE of THREE independent expert auditors (Sol, Fable, K3), working BLIND to each other. Full
access to everything; search the web at full capability. Be concrete, decisive, line-by-line.

## The product + metric
An autonomous deep-research agent composes a long cited report, scored by DeepResearch Bench RACE
(4 dims: Comprehensiveness, Insight, Instruction-Following, Readability; judge openai/gpt-5.5).

## The two runs to COMPARE (same pipeline + same generator Kimi K3 + same B/E/F levers; the ONLY
difference is PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS)
- CHAMPION = K3 + B/E/F (route-all OFF). RACE mean 0.5084. dir: outputs/k3_b1_run/
- ROUTE-ALL = K3 + B/E/F + PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1. RACE mean 0.5023 (WORSE by -0.0061). dir:
  outputs/k3_b1_routeall_run/
Per-dim (champion -> route-all): Comprehensiveness 0.5231->0.5177 (-0.005), Insight 0.5252->0.5159
(-0.009), Instruction 0.5079->0.5008 (-0.007), Readability 0.4567->0.4654 (+0.009). Net NEGATIVE.

## THE PUZZLE (start here)
route-all routes EVERY home-less "orphan" consolidated basket + unassigned high-tier singleton into
the section whose title+focus shares the most topical content words with it (code:
verified_compose.py:3837 route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans; called multi_section_generator.py
:11251). Intuition: MORE evidence per section => MORE comprehensive. REALITY: the route-all report is
SMALLER (68,493 vs 76,111 bytes; 8,343 vs 9,368 words; 135 vs 144 unique citations) AND scores LOWER
on Comprehensiveness AND Insight — despite a LARGER reasoning trace (405,425 vs 386,231 bytes = MORE
composition work). More routing + more work -> less output + lower score. WHY?

## Hypotheses to confirm/refute (don't just accept — verify in the artifacts + code)
1. Dilution: adding low-value orphan baskets to a section makes the K3 section-writer (rule 8:
   "as many source-anchored sentences as the evidence supports with DISTINCT decision-relevant
   facts, and no more") judge the enlarged pool as less focused -> writes FEWER distinct sentences.
2. Displacement: appending orphan ev_ids to section.ev_ids changes basket selection / ordering so
   HIGH-value baskets get crowded out, truncated, or de-prioritized (token/section budget).
3. Residual absorption: content diverted into the "Additional Corroborated Findings" keep-all
   residual section (which the render may drop or the judge may discount).
4. Off-topic / mis-routing: an orphan routed by shallow content-word overlap lands in the WRONG
   section, adding noise the judge penalizes (Insight/Instruction).
5. Budget/truncation: more baskets -> per-section token budget exhausted -> sections truncated.
6. Something else you find.

## Materials (read LINE BY LINE, both runs, and DIFF them)
- Reports: outputs/k3_b1_run/report.md  vs  outputs/k3_b1_routeall_run/report.md
- Reasoning traces (per-section compose decisions, tokens, drops): reasoning_trace.jsonl (both)
- Outlines (section plans + ev_id assignments): multi_section_outline.json (both)
- Bibliographies: bibliography.json (both)
- compose_summary.json (both)
- Run logs: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/k3_b1_run.log  vs  k3_b1_routeall_run.log
- RACE per-dim: third_party/deep_research_bench/results/race/{k3b1race_r1..r3, rarace_r1..r3}/race_result.txt
- Code: verified_compose.py (route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans ~3837-3960, _section_baskets_for_compose ~3342, _extend_plan_ev_ids); multi_section_generator.py (the _call_section legacy path ~3979-6560, section evidence gathering, PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET ~588, PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS ~716, the residual section, :11251 route call); the SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_FIELD_AGNOSTIC (~3708) incl. rule 8.

## Deliverable (structured, no preamble)
The ROOT CAUSE of the degradation, proven with specific evidence (quote the report diffs / trace
lines / code:line). Rank contributing causes. Then: is route-all SALVAGEABLE with a GENERAL fix
(e.g. a novelty/marginal-coverage gate before routing, a per-section evidence cap, better overlap
scoring), or is force-routing orphans fundamentally wrong for a quality-judged report? Any fix MUST
be GENERAL (works for any domain/RQ), central-config-gated, NO task/benchmark literals, NO
hard-coded values, NO overfit, plain names. End with your single #1 root cause + one-line verdict:
KEEP route-all off / FIX it (name the fix) / DROP the mechanism.

content.content:
1:# A literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market
5:## Introduction: AI, the Fourth Industrial Revolution, and Labor Markets
9:## Theoretical Foundations: Task-Based Models of Displacement and Reinstatement
13:## Occupational Exposure: Quantifying Susceptibility to AI and Generative AI
17:## Productivity and Augmentation: Evidence from Firms and Controlled Experiments
21:## Employment and Wage Dynamics: Displacement, Creation, and Polarization
25:## Skill Transformation, Job Quality, and the Future of Work
29:## Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions
33:## Conclusions and Research Gaps
37:## Limitations
41:## References
